Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Matthew S. Sepuya of McGrane PC Tel. No. 1-415-282-4807 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. George C. Harris & Atty. Elizabeth Balassone of Morrison Foerster,
Tel. No. 1-415-268-7328 appearing for the Defendant.
RULING - Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, discloses that he served as the settlement judge in the "Government Enforcement" case brought by the City, but he does not believe that disqualifies him from deciding motions in this case. The matter was argued. Plaintiffs Bennett Goldberg and Linda Kuckuk's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is granted in part. Defendant Stephens Institute is required to: a) provide further responses to interrogatories 2 and 3 that relate to and cover the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017; and b) at its option, either produce the documents required to be produced in response to request 13 as set forth in the order on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents or provide further responses to interrogatory 1 limited to the most recent contact information for each person who signed a bed license between May 13, 2015 and March 30, 2017. The Academy is not required to provide further responses to interrogatories 6 and 7. The portions of this motion that are denied are being denied without prejudice to plaintiffs requesting the documents requested or some subset thereof at a later date on a fuller showing of discovery relevance than has been made in the moving papers. Plaintiffs have not shown that there is any arguable viability to their delayed discovery allegations and their requests for considerable amount of information outside the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017 without limitation as to time are overbroad and lacking in discovery relevance. Plaintiffs have not shown that the identities of the owners of the LLCs meet the standard of discovery relevance. Responsive information to interrogatory 1 needs to be provided without need for a notice to any of the putative class members. Further responses are due no later than August 6, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Atty. Matthew S. Sepuya of McGrane PC Tel. No. 1-415-282-4807 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. George C. Harris & Atty. Elizabeth Balassone of Morrison Foerster,
Tel. No. 1-415-268-7328 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, discloses that he served as the settlement judge in the "Government Enforcement case" brought by the City, but he does not believe that disqualifies him from deciding motions in this case. The matter was argued. Plaintiffs Bennett Goldberg and Linda Kuckuk's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents is granted in part. Defendant Stephens Institute is required to: a) provide further responses and produce all documents responsive to requests 2 and 3; b) provide further responses and produce documents responsive to requests 1, 6, 12 that relate to and cover the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017; and c) at its option, either respond to special interrogatory 1 as set forth in the order on plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories or provide further responses and produce documents responsive to request 13 limited to the most recent document concerning each person who signed a bed license between May 13, 2015 and March 30, 2017 that contains contact information for that person and all non-contact information about that person may be redacted. The Academy is not required to provide further responses or produce any documents as to requests 4 and 5. The portions of this motion that are denied are being denied without prejudice to plaintiffs requesting the documents requested or some subset thereof at a later date on a fuller showing of discovery relevance than has been made in the moving papers. Plaintiffs have not shown that there is any arguable viability to their delayed discovery allegations and their requests for many documents outside the time period May 13, 2015 through March 30, 2017 without limitation as to time are overbroad and lacking in discovery relevance. All documents regarding the LLCs' acquisition and transfer of interests in the buildings that are the subject of this case meet the standard of discovery relevance. Plaintiffs have not shown that the identities of the owners of the LLCs or the formations of the LLCs meet the standard of discovery relevance. Request 13 has been limited to require only contact information of the putative class and such information needs to be provided without need for a notice to any of the putative class members. Further responses are due no later than August 6, 2017. Request for Production 2 and 3 are overbroad and they are not limited to transaction with the academy and involved a chain of title. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and was allowed to modify the order in accordance with the argument this morning and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories 12.1 To 12.7
Off Calendar Jun-06-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Matthew S. Sepuya of McGrane PC Tel. No. 1-415-282-4807 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. George C. Harris & Atty. Elizabeth Balassone of Morrison Foerster,
Tel. No. 1-415-268-7328 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - Judge Kahn, who is hearing this motion, discloses that he served as the settlement judge in the "Government Enforcement" case brought by the City, but he does not believe that disqualifies him from deciding motions in this case. The matter was argued. Defendant Stephens Institute's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 12.1 through 12.7 is granted in its entirety. Plaintiffs Bennett Goldberg and Linda Kuckuk are required to provide further responses to each of these interrogatories by construing the word "incident" to cover all allegations of wrongdoing that they have alleged against the Academy. The word "incident" and the information called for in these interrogatories are sufficiently expansive to cover the allegations of wrongdoing in the complaint. Further responses are due no later than July 6, 2017. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
